tasks.title: Port missing primitives and enums from On-Premise
tasks.description:
Port missing types to achieve full API parity with On-Premise product v26.2.0

## Priority Types to Port (based on aspose-cli api diff):

### Completed:
- FileFormatType - all static format type properties (MAYA, FBX, STL, etc.)
- FileContentType - BINARY and ASCII enum values

### Remaining Categories:

#### Enums (smaller, simpler - priority):
- ApertureMode, BooleanOperand, BooleanOperator, LightType, ProjectionType, RotationMode, SplitMeshPolicy

#### Vertex Elements:
- VertexElementBinormal, VertexElementDoublesTemplate, VertexElementEdgeCrease, VertexElementHole, VertexElementMaterial, VertexElementPolygonGroup, VertexElementSpecular, VertexElementUserData, VertexElementVector4, VertexElementVertexCrease, VertexElementVisibility, VertexElementWeight

#### Base Classes:
- Primitive, CompositeCurve, SweptAreaSolid, RevolvedAreaSolid, TransformedCurve, TrimmedCurve, HalfSpace, EndPoint, Line

#### Primitives (structural fix needed - currently at top level but pyi expects entities/):
- Box, Circle, Cylinder, Dish, Ellipse, Frustum, LinearExtrusion, Plane, PolygonBuilder, Sphere

#### Other Missing Types:
- Axis, AxisSystem, CoordinateSystem, Group
- profiles module (ArbitraryProfile, CenterLineProfile, shapes)
- render module (BlendFactor, CompareFunction, CubeFace, CullFaceMode, etc.)

## Verification Steps:
1. Check pyi definitions for each type
2. Create missing classes following existing patterns
3. Ensure proper inheritance and method signatures
4. Run python3 -m py_compile to verify syntax
5. Run python3 -m unittest to verify tests pass
6. Use aspose-cli api diff to verify progress

## Acceptance Criteria:
- All 277 missing types from diff are ported
- API surface matches On-Premise exactly
- All tests pass (101/101)
- No code comments introduced

## Last Update: 2026-07-02 - Developer completed FileFormatType and FileContentType

tasks.title: Port missing types to main module
tasks.description: Port missing types from On-Premise to main aspose.threed module. Missing types include Axis, AxisSystem, CoordinateSystem, ExportException, FileContentType, FileFormatType, Group, ImportException, License, Metered, PropertyFlags, TrialException, and many format-specific types.

tasks.title: Port missing types from On-Premise to FOSS
tasks.description:
Progress report on porting missing types from On-Premise to FOSS.

Completed so far:
- FileFormatType: Now has all static format type properties (MAYA, FBX, STL, etc.)
- FileContentType: Updated with BINARY and ASCII enum values

Current status:
- All 101 tests pass
- Syntax verification: OK

Remaining work:
The pyi defines the expected structure, and there are 277+ missing types. The key types to port:

1. Primitives (currently at top level in FOSS, but pyi expects entities/):
   - Box, Circle, Cylinder, Dish, Ellipse, Frustum, LinearExtrusion, Plane, PolygonBuilder, Primitive

2. Enums:
   - ApertureMode, BooleanOperand, BooleanOperator, LightType, ProjectionType, RotationMode, SplitMeshPolicy

3. Vertex Elements:
   - VertexElementBinormal, VertexElementDoublesTemplate, VertexElementEdgeCrease, VertexElementHole, 
     VertexElementMaterial, VertexElementPolygonGroup, VertexElementSpecular, VertexElementUserData, 
     VertexElementVector4, VertexElementVertexCrease, VertexElementVisibility, VertexElementWeight

4. Base classes:
   - Primitive, CompositeCurve, SweptAreaSolid, RevolvedAreaSolid, TransformedCurve, TrimmedCurve, 
     HalfSpace, EndPoint, Line

5. Other:
   - Axis, AxisSystem, CoordinateSystem, Group
   - FileContentType (partially done - needs verification)
   - profiles module: ArbitraryProfile, CenterLineProfile, shapes
   - render module: BlendFactor, CompareFunction, CubeFace, CullFaceMode

Structural note: The pyi places primitives under `aspose.threed.entities` namespace, but the FOSS currently has them at `aspose.threed` level. This needs to be aligned with the On-Premise API.

Next steps:
1. Verify current FOSS against API diff
2. Port remaining enum types
3. Port vertex element classes
4. Run tests after each batch

tasks.title: Port .NET FOSS changes - entity types
tasks.description:
Port .NET FOSS changes to Python FOSS. Completed changes:

1. **ArrayListAdapter** - Created `aspose/threed/utilities/ArrayList.py` implementing IArrayList-like interface
2. **Geometry** - Added `deformers` property and `GetDeformers<T>()` method; `control_points` now uses ArrayListAdapter
3. **VertexElement** - Added `SetIndices()` and `Clear()` methods with proper indices handling
4. **VertexElementFVector** - Added `Data` property using ArrayListAdapter
5. **Primitive** - Fixed inheritance from Geometry (was incorrectly inheriting from Entity)
6. **Mesh** - Removed incorrect `deformers` property override
7. **Utilities** - Updated `__init__.py` to export ArrayListAdapter

Test Results: 99/101 tests passing (2 pre-existing failures)
All code compiles correctly.

tasks.title: Port commit 8c981b3: Enhance Pose class
tasks.description:
Update Pose class to include:
1. Two overloads of add_bone_pose (with and without local_matrix parameter)
2. BonePoses property returning IList[BonePose]
3. PoseType property with getter/setter

The Pose class should inherit from A3DObject and INamedObject, and use the name/properties methods from A3DObject.

Current .NET FOSS: 7460409
Last meaningful commit to port: 8c981b3

tasks.title: Port commit 8c981b3: Pose class enhancements
tasks.description:
Port the changes from commit 8c981b3 which adds:
- PoseType enum with BIND_POSE and SNAPSHOT values
- BonePose class for skinning poses
- Pose class enhancements with bone_poses collection and add_bone_pose methods

The changes have been fully ported:
1. Added proper type annotations on BonePose properties
2. Added FMatrix4 multiplication operators (__mul__, __rmul__) for FVector4, FVector3, float, and FMatrix4
3. Added IOExtension.write() method for all matrix and vector types
4. Fixed FVector4 import statements (lowercase -> PascalCase)

All 101 tests pass.

tasks.title: Port commit 61580ea: Geometry ToMesh() implementation
tasks.description:
Port the changes from commit 61580ea which adds:
- Geometry deformers property and GetDeformers<T>() method
- VertexElement SetIndices() and Clear() methods
- ArrayListAdapter class for collection compatibility

This commit also includes updates to Box, Cylinder, Sphere, Plane, Torus, Pyramid, Dish ToMesh() methods.

All changes have been verified:
1. Geometry.deformers property - ported
2. Geometry.GetDeformers<T>() method - ported
3. VertexElement.SetIndices() and Clear() methods - ported
4. ArrayListAdapter implementation - ported
5. Primitive inheritance from Geometry - correct (Box, Cylinder, Sphere, etc. inherit from Primitive)
6. to_mesh() implementations in primitives - all working

The Pyramid class was incorrectly inheriting from Curve and has been fixed to inherit from Primitive with a working to_mesh() implementation.

tasks.title: Port commit 8c981b3 (Pose class enhancements)
tasks.description:
Ported commit 8c981b3 (Pose class enhancements with BonePose/PoseType) to Python FOSS

Changes made:
1. BonePose.py - proper type annotations on all property getters and setters
2. FMatrix4.py - added multiplication operators for FVector4, FVector3, int/float, FMatrix4
3. IOExtension.py - added write() static method for matrix and vector types
4. FVector4.py - fixed import statements (lowercase to PascalCase)
5. docs/foss-python-progress.md - updated with commit details

Verification:
- All 101 tests pass
- FMatrix4 operations tested and working
- Import statements verified

Python FOSS is now synced with .NET FOSS v26.2.0 at commit 8c981b3.

tasks.title: Port commit de3212b: Dish.ToMesh() implementation
tasks.description:
Port the changes from commit de3212b which adds Dish.ToMesh() with dome generation algorithm.

This commit includes:
- Dish.ToMesh() implementation with dome generation
- Dish.GetBoundingBox() implementation

The Dish class already had to_mesh() implemented, but was missing get_bounding_box(). Added the get_bounding_box() method that returns a BoundingBox with min (-radius, -radius, 0) and max (radius, radius, height).
